subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The capacity of an acid neutralising tank must be authorised by the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank and fixed baffles must be constructed of a minimum—

ol.li: 0.63 mm acid resistant stainless steel all welded construction; or
other authorised acid and alkali resistant material.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank must contain three equally spaced baffles.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank must be manufactured with—

ol.li: the diameter of the outlet pipe being at least one size larger than the inlet pipe (i.e. DN 65 inlet - DN 80 outlet); or
where the inlet pipe is DN 100, the diameter of the outlet may be DN 100.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The outlet of an acid neutralising tank must include a 32 mm x 32 mm x 25 mm deep PVC-U test block bracket fitted as near as practicable to the outlet.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank must include a 150 mm x 25 mm x 25 mm cement mortar test block fitted to the satisfaction of the Permit Authority.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank must include marble chips in the 40 mm to 60 mm size range.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: An acid neutralising tank must be located in a position that—

ol.li: enables ready access for maintenance and inspection; and
provides ready access for fitting of the cement mortar test block; and
enables ready access to the sampling valve; and
provides ready access for replacement of the marble chips.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: When an acid neutralising tank is to be located below ground level, it must—

ol.li: be surrounded with concrete walls and base with a minimum thickness of 100 mm; and
omit the 25 mm sampling valve; and
have a minimum DN 100 outlet pipe located no deeper than 600 mm below surface level; and
have the outlet pipe connected to a disconnector gully fitted with a loose fitting grate or screwed cap.

callout.p: The requirements of C4D17(4) does not apply to existing systems.
The capacity of an acid neutralising tank as shown in Tas figure C4D17 is the minimum requirement of 27 litres between the marble chip level and the outlet level.
See TAS Figure C4D17 for an acid neutralising tank.
